title: Fluffy German Custard Skillet
description: A traditional German dish featuring a jammy and tender custard, perfect for a comforting sick day meal, made in a cast-iron skillet and suitable for low-glycemic diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, sugar, vanilla extract, and salt until well combined.
Add the melted butter, lemon zest, and whisk until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a 10-inch cast-iron skillet.
Dollop the raspberry jam on top of the custard mixture.
Place the skillet in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the skillet.
Bake for 35 minutes or until the edges are set and the center is still slightly jiggly.
Remove the skillet from the water bath and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Serve warm, garnished with additional lemon zest if desired.
